subject: Re: Guest Lecture — Draft Outline
body:
Dear Prof. Meyers,

I've been putting together a rough outline for the September 16 lecture. Here is what I am thinking so far:

1. Opening (10 min): What community reference actually means — beyond the desk
2. Case studies from Welles-Turner (25 min): outreach to seniors, ESL reference, the genealogy program
3. Reference interview technique: live demonstration with a student volunteer (20 min)
4. Open discussion (15 min)

Does this match what you had in mind for the course arc? Happy to adjust.

Jenny

subject: Re: Garden Club Newsletter — My Notes on Native Ferns
body:
Ruth,

I wanted to add a short note to go alongside Dorothy's fern piece. Here is a draft paragraph you can use or edit:

"For north-facing beds with dry summer shade, Jenny Wilkins of Winding Brook Lane recommends Dryopteris marginalis (Marginal Wood Fern) and Polystichum acrostichoides (Christmas Fern) as the most forgiving choices for Connecticut gardens. Both are deer-resistant and hold their fronds well into December."

Feel free to shorten it as needed.

Jenny

subject: Questions for October 14 Wellness Visit
body:
Dr. Park,

Ahead of our October 14 appointment I wanted to note the topics I hope to discuss:

1. Raynaud's — noticeably worse last winter; want to ask about heated vest options and whether there's a referral to vascular warranted
2. GERD — Omeprazole seems to be working but I'd like to confirm the dose is still appropriate
3. Osteoarthritis — hands were stiff most of April and May; ask about whether glucosamine dose is right
4. General: blood pressure trend review

Would it be helpful to email you my current medication list in advance?

Jenny Wilkins

subject: Re: Sunday Update — A Few Garden Photos
body:
Claire,

Harriet took some photos this morning of the 'June' hosta blooms — I'll attach them when I send this. Three full flowers open at once, and the chartreuse hearts are just stunning in the morning light. I thought Bea might enjoy them for her nature journal if she keeps one.

Also wanted to say: the marine camp news is wonderful. Mystic Aquarium was one of our favorite places when you and Thomas were young. She'll be in the right environment.

See you Sunday on the call,
Jenny

subject: Re: Book Sale — Fiction Cart Idea
body:
James,

I had an idea for the July sale: what if we set up a small "Staff Picks" shelf at the front of the fiction section? Just 10–15 books with handwritten tags explaining why we liked them — the way the library used to do it. Might encourage browsers to pick up something they wouldn't normally consider.

I'm happy to write 5–6 tags if you and Dorothy each do a few. Let me know what you think.

Jenny

subject: Inquiry — Autumn Fern Availability
body:
Tom,

When I come in Saturday for the hosta pickup, I also want to ask about Dryopteris erythrosora (Autumn Fern) — the one with the copper new growth. I'm looking for two good-sized plants for a shady spot along my east fence. Do you typically carry those, or would it need to be a special order like the hostas?

Thank you,
Jenny

subject: Book Club Discussion Questions — A Gentleman in Moscow
body:
Audrey and Dorothy,

Here is a first pass at discussion questions for A Gentleman in Moscow. Feel free to trim or reorder:

1. How does Towles use confinement as a lens for freedom?
2. The Count's relationship with Sofia — what does it reveal about chosen family?
3. What role does food and ritual play in maintaining dignity under constraint?
4. The ending — earned resolution or too tidy? Discuss.
5. Which character beside the Count surprised you most, and why?

I was also going to suggest we pick our September book at the August meeting — last year we waited too long and everyone was scrambling. Thoughts?

Jenny

subject: Webster Bank — Question About June Statement
body:
Harriet,

I noticed on the June Webster Bank statement that there's a charge for $47.50 I don't recognize — it shows as "WBST SVC CHG" on June 1. Do you know what this is? I want to call the bank if it's an error.

Phone is 1-800-325-2424 if we need to call together.

Jenny

subject: Additional Item for Thursday — Screen Door
body:
Phil,

While you're here Thursday for the porch steps, I wanted to ask if you'd also have time to look at the back screen door — the latch has been sticking badly since March and Harriet nearly walked through it last week. It may just need the screws tightened or a replacement latch plate. Probably 20–30 minutes of additional work?

Let me know if that's feasible.

Jenny

subject: Tuesday — Can We Also Swing by the Library?
body:
Peggy,

After coffee Tuesday would you be up for a quick walk to the library? I need to pick up The Covenant of Water before my hold expires on June 20, and it would be nice company. We could take the long way back along Hubbard Street if the weather holds.

Jenny

subject: Re: Thursday Coffee — Confirming Daybreak
body:
Elena,

Let's say Thursday, June 19, 10:30 AM at Daybreak Bakery, Glastonbury (they're right on Main Street near the library). I'll get there a bit early to grab a table — they fill up fast on Thursday mornings.

Very much looking forward to catching up.

Jenny

subject: Re: Summer Community Potluck — RSVP
body:
Dear Susan,

Harriet and I will plan to come to the June 28 potluck — please count us in. I'll bring a pot of my butternut squash soup (mild, dairy-free, great cold-evening food) and a loaf of whole grain bread.

Looking forward to seeing everyone.

Jenny Wilkins
48 Winding Brook Lane, Glastonbury

subject: Re: Library Board Recognition — July 11
body:
Dear Margaret,

Thank you again. I've been thinking about what to say at the July 11 recognition and I'd like to use it as a chance to thank the Friends volunteers specifically — they are the ones who make the sale work year after year. I'll keep it under two minutes.

Is there anything the board would like me to include?

Jenny

subject: Book Sale — Donation Box Labels
body:
James,

I'll print the donation box labels on my printer before July 11 if you can send me the text you want on them. Last year we had "FICTION — A through L" etc. — same system this year, or do you want to try sorting by genre instead?

Let me know and I'll have them ready by July 9.

Jenny

subject: Garden Club — Pot Division Reminder for Members
body:
Ruth,

For the newsletter or a separate email to members before the November swap — could we include a reminder that pot divisions for the plant swap should be in proper nursery pots (or at least labeled clearly) by November 3 so they have time to settle before the swap on the 7th?

Last year a few people brought root divisions wrapped in newspaper and they weren't in the best shape. Just a friendly nudge.

Jenny

subject: Renewal Request — The Covenant of Water
body:
Hello,

I'd like to request a renewal for my hold copy of The Covenant of Water by Abraham Verghese (currently due June 20, 2026). I was unable to pick it up this week — would it be possible to extend the hold by 7 days?

Library card number: WTL-004829

Thank you,
Jenny Wilkins
860-555-0100

subject: Re: Formal Invitation — Thank You, Parking Note
body:
Dear Prof. Meyers,

Thank you for the formal letter and for arranging the parking space in Lot C — that is very thoughtful. I'll plan to arrive by 1:45 PM to set up before the 2:00 start.

One small request: if there is a chalkboard or whiteboard in Room 114, I'd like to use it for the reference interview demonstration. If not, a flip chart would work fine. Could you check?

Thank you again,
Jenny

subject: Question About Omeprazole Generic Brand
body:
Dear Karen,

Quick question about the Omeprazole refill ready for pickup: is this the same generic brand as last time (the blue-and-white capsule)? I had some mild throat irritation with a different brand a couple of years ago and just want to confirm before I pick it up.

Thank you,
Jenny Wilkins
860-555-0100

subject: Book Club — September Pick Idea
body:
Dorothy,

I know we haven't had the August meeting yet, but I've been thinking about the September pick and wanted to float an idea: Lessons in Chemistry by Bonnie Garmus. It's warm and funny, very readable, and I think it will generate good discussion about women, work, and identity — themes our group tends to love.

Shall I suggest it to Audrey, or would you like to?

Jenny

subject: Tonight's Dinner — Butternut Squash Soup?
body:
Harriet,

I was thinking for tonight: butternut squash soup with the whole grain bread from Tuesday, and maybe some mild cheddar on the side? It's a cool evening and I still have a good squash in the pantry from the farmers' market.

Let me know by 3 if you have other ideas — I'll start the soup at 4:30 otherwise.

Jenny

subject: Re: Plant Swap — Table Count Confirmation
body:
Michael,

After checking with Ruth, we're expecting approximately 28 members for the November 7 plant swap. If you can set up 8 six-foot tables in the Main Hall that would be ideal — 6 for plant display and 2 for registration and refreshments near the entrance.

Thank you again for accommodating us,
Jenny Wilkins
Glastonbury Garden Club

subject: Fidelity Statement — IRA Balance Check
body:
Harriet,

The Fidelity quarterly statement is available online — can you log in and check our brokerage balance when you get a chance? I also want to confirm the IRA allocations haven't drifted too far. Last time we looked the bond tilt was about 60/40 — I just want to make sure it hasn't shifted.

No rush, just before end of June.

Jenny
